key_insight: This research presents a novel framework integrating multiphysics phase-field simulation for real-time structural health monitoring of SCFRP composites through piezoresistive characteristics.
engineering_relevance: The framework enables accurate damage assessment in composite materials, significantly enhancing their reliability and maintenance in engineering applications.
method: Integration of viscoelastic-viscoplastic constitutive and anisotropic crack resistance models.
Use of an artificial neural network for damage inference from electrical impedance data.
evidence_signal: The artificial neural network achieves an R2 of 0.99 on held-out configurations, confirming its predictive accuracy across various microstructures.
watch_item: The complexity of real-time implementation in diverse environmental conditions may affect the accuracy of damage assessments.
paper_title: Data-Driven Structural Health Monitoring of Short Carbon Fiber-Reinforced Polymer Composites via Multiphysics Phase-Field Simulation
